mission.title: perses: [ENHANCEMENT] Unify loading and error handling for all panels, pass data as props to panels
mission.description:
# Description

* **unify loading and error handling for all panels:** Move the loading and error handling from panel plugins to parent `<Panel>` component. This reduces the responsibility of the panel plugins to only render the chart, and not having to handle the loading and error states individually.
* **add loading and error indicator to panel header:** This resolves the issue of swallowed errors if one query succeeds and another query fails (see removed comment in `TimeSeriesChartPanel.tsx`) for all panels. Now the successful query is rendered, and a warning indicator shows the error of the failing query.
* **pass query data as props to panel plugins:** This enables embedding a Panel in another Panel, for example the table panel can embed the TimeSeriesChartPanel in its cells. It also simplifies testing of panel plugins.

It uses the following algorithm to determine the panel content:
1. If any query has data available, render the panel.
2. Otherwise, if any query is in loading stat

resolution.summary:
# Description
This PR introduces a new feature that allows the use of label metrics as values in the Stat Chart.

Resolves #2340 

# Screenshots

![Screenshot 2025-01-20 at 17 28 45](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/25079ffb-440c-45f3-899e-e8ab28dcb1d1)

# Checklist

- [x] Pull request has a descriptive title and context useful to a reviewer.
- [x] Pull request title follows the `[<catalog_entry>] <commit message>` naming convention using one of the
  following `catalog_entry` values: `FEATURE`, `ENHANCEMENT`, `BUGFIX`, `BREAKINGCHANGE`, `DOC`,`IGNORE`.
- [x] All commits have [DCO signoffs](https://github.com/probot/dco#how-it-works).

## UI Changes

- [x] Changes that impact the UI include screenshots and/or screencasts of the relevant changes.
- [x] Code follows the [UI guidelines](https://github.com/perses/perses/blob/main/ui/ui-guidelines.md).
- [x] Visual tests are stable and unlikely to be flaky.
  See [Storybook](https://github.com/perses/perses/tree/main/ui/storybook#visual-tests)
  and [e2e](https://github.com/perses/perses/tree/main/ui/e2e#visual-tests) docs for more details. Common issues
  include:
  - Is the data inconsistent? You need to mock API requests.
  - Does the time change? You need to use consistent time values or mock time utilities.
  - Does it have loading states? You need to wait for loading to complete.
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
